WARNING: THIS GAME ONLY WORKS IF YOU DO THE FOLLOWING
1) After downloading the game and BEFORE installation you HAVE to download Ubisoft Connect, create an account and enter the game's activation key. Otherwise when you go to install the game you'll run into a Ubisoft network error. 

2) Install the game, then close it.

3) Go to the Subsim website and download the Wolves of Steel super mod and self installer. Make sure the mod is installed in the same directory as Silent Hunter 5.

4) Start the game. If you start it and it shows up in a window in the corner of your screen with no audio and response; then just let the video play all the way till the end and then close steam, if that doesn't remove the window then restart your computer.

5) Go to the Silent Hunter .exe location, usually found in c:/steam/steamapps/common/Silent Hunter 5. 
Right-click  SH5.exe, select Properties, select the Compatibility tab, and then select the Disable display scaling on high DPI settings check box.

6) Run the game and enjoy.

AGAIN, this game is completely unplayable without all these steps so avoid it completely if you are unwilling or unable to do all of them. Ubisoft released a broken game and never patched or fixed it properly but thankfully the diligent and talented folks over at Subsim created a brilliant megamod that not only makes the game work for once but also creates a rich, immersive experience that's unequaled in other sub simulators.
Terrible, abandoned, do not buy.

Buy Silent Hunter Wolves of the Pacific.
In SH5, you are the  first-person  captain and move around your sub. You have to do everything by the letter. There is no shortcut. For example, if you need to dive or crash dive, and you're on the deck, well.. you need to move first, go down the stairs, then you can dive. You can't change direction when backing up. There is no easy way to change the bearing without tracing it on the map. Defining the depth is a challenge as the control is very small. Shooting torpedoes is also a challenge, even in automatic mode. Need to check with the Observation then Attack periscopes? That's easy.. run dand climb the stairs to switch. Add other bugs in the game with the mouse. Time compressing sucks. For example, if you're travelling long range and encounter a DD, time-compressing will be set to 32, still too fast and all you can see is the damage done to the sub, or miss completely a convoy. Exiting time-compressing should have been reset to 1.
Sure, Silent Hunter Wolves of the Pacific is buggy.. But at least, it does not have all of these non-sense.

Unsurprisingly, the game is a piece of sh*t without TWoS (thanks Ubi), HOWEVER it is well worth ten bucks if you can get the mod working, IMO better than SH3 GWX or SH4. TWoS mod improves performance, overhauls the UI, improves the sound, gives the game more tools, and drastically expands the game's content.
Better graphics etc. all in all it's like the movie "Das Boot"  which in German means "the boat"...it's almost exactly like the movie, right down to maximum depth you can bring your sub.

I've played this game for 250.6 hours, and here are the issues I've run into. Tonnage, and number of ships sunk is not recorded in the missions in the Campaign.cfg file, despite the ships sunk is recorded in the CampaignProgess.cfg file. I have resorted to recording the ships sunk and tonnage in a separate worksheet, like U-boat captains in the old days. I then go into Campaign.cfg, after I save and exit, then update the tonnage and number of ships sunk, and change “false” to “true” for each mission completed in the campaign. I am forced to use the silentotto command to move from one campaign to the next. Another problem is diving. If I go beyond 50 meters, the ship keeps diving, until I blow ballast, and reset the depth above where I am rising from.  For this reason I have to run the game with unlimited compressed air. 

Ubisoft should be ashamed for putting this game out with so many problems, and not issuing a patch for it. Their quality as a game company has dropped sharply since the days I played Rogue Spear and Splinter Cell. Glad I only paid $10 for it. I think with these issues even that price is too much. Save your money, and play Silent Hunter 4 instead, or play something new.

Be prepared to spend hours learning the most basic aspects as you get to grips with one of the best subsims ever created.
Yes it's old and buggy, and needs some essential mods (wolves of Steel) but it's a great game.
You control a sub and must manage almost every aspect. Careful planning of your movements to get yourself into conveys undetected, then launching your attack before quickly taking evasive actions. Fantasticly tense action interspersed with periods of more mundane navigation.
Thank god you never had to go through this for real in ww2. Silent hunter 5 is as real as it gets. Fantastic (if it works)
